title: tetradymia axillaris
intro: Tetradymia axillaris, also known as White Horseweed or Rocky Mountain White Horseweed, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It belongs to the daisy family and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is rich in flavonoids, sesquiterpenoids, and other bioactive compounds that contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting immune function. Tetradymia axillaris can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or salve, and is often used in herbal remedies to treat various health conditions, including skin issues, respiratory problems, and digestive issues.
